vue/require-v-for-key.message: Elements in iteration expect to have 'v-bind:key' directives. Element: <{tag}>
vue/require-v-for-key.help:
**Why:** The `:key` attribute helps Vue's virtual DOM efficiently track and update list items. **Fix:** Add a unique identifier as the key: ```vue
  • {{ item.name }}
    • ``` **Best practices:** - Use unique, stable identifiers (like database IDs) - Avoid using array index as key when list order may change - Keys must be primitive values (string or number)
vue/valid-v-for.invalid_syntax: v-for has invalid syntax: expected 'item in items' or '(item, index) in items'
vue/valid-v-for.help:
**Valid v-for syntax:** ```vue
  • {{ item }}
  • {{ index }}: {{ item }}
  • {{ key }}: {{ value }}
  • {{ index }}
    • {{ n }} ```
vue/valid-v-if.help:
**Fix:** Add a boolean expression: ```vue
Content
Has items
``` **Note:** Use `v-show` for frequent toggling (better performance), `v-if` for conditions that rarely change.
vue/valid-v-else.missing_v_if: v-else requires a corresponding v-if or v-else-if
vue/valid-v-else.help:
**Correct usage:** ```vue
A
B
Other
``` **Rules:** - `v-else` takes no expression - Must immediately follow `v-if` or `v-else-if` - No other elements between them
vue/valid-v-bind.help:
**Fix:** Provide a value to bind: ```vue
```
vue/valid-v-on.help:
**Fix:** Specify the event name:
```vue
<!-- Shorthand -->
<button @click="handleClick">Click</button>

<!-- Full syntax -->
<button v-on:click="handleClick">Click</button>

<!-- With modifiers -->
<form @submit.prevent="onSubmit">...</form>
<input @keyup.enter="onEnter">
```
vue/valid-v-model.invalid_element: v-model cannot be used on <{tag}> element
vue/valid-v-model.help:
**Supported elements:**
- `<input>` (text, checkbox, radio, etc.)
- `<textarea>`
- `<select>`
- Custom components with `modelValue` prop

**Examples:**
```vue
<input v-model="message">
<input v-model.number="age" type="number">
<input v-model.trim="name">
<MyComponent v-model="value">
```
vue/valid-v-show.on_template: v-show cannot be used on <template> element
vue/valid-v-show.help: Add an expression to the v-show directive
vue/valid-v-slot.invalid_location: v-slot can only be used on components or template elements
vue/valid-v-slot.help: Use v-slot on a component or a template element inside a component
vue/no-use-v-if-with-v-for.description: Disallow using v-if on the same element as v-for
vue/no-use-v-if-with-v-for.message_acces: Avoid using v-if with v-for on the same element. The v-if condition does not have access to v-for scope variables
vue/no-use-v-if-with-v-for.message_perf: Avoid using v-if with v-for on the same element. Use a computed property to filter the list first
vue/no-use-v-if-with-v-for.help: **Problem:** `v-for` has higher priority than `v-if`, causing the entire list to iterate before filtering. **Solution 1:** Use a computed property: ```vue ``` **Solution 2:** Wrap with `
vue/no-unused-vars.description: Disallow unused variable definitions in v-for directives
vue/no-unused-vars.message: Variable '{name}' is defined but never used
vue/no-unused-vars.help:
**Options:**

1. Use the variable in your template
2. Remove unused variables:
```vue
<!-- Before -->
<li v-for="(item, index) in items" :key="item.id">

<!-- After (if index is unused) -->
<li v-for="item in items" :key="item.id">
```

3. Prefix with `_` to indicate intentional non-use:
```vue
<li v-for="(_item, index) in items" :key="index">
```
vue/no-template-key.description: Disallow key attribute on template element
vue/no-template-key.message: <template> elements cannot have a key attribute
vue/no-template-key.help: Move the key to the first child element inside template
vue/no-textarea-mustache.message: Mustache interpolation inside textarea is not allowed
vue/no-textarea-mustache.help: Use v-model instead of mustache interpolation in textarea
vue/no-dupe-v-else-if.description: Disallow duplicate conditions in v-if chains
vue/no-dupe-v-else-if.message: This condition is a duplicate of a condition in the same v-if/v-else-if chain
vue/no-dupe-v-else-if.help: Remove or modify the duplicate condition
vue/no-reserved-component-names.message: '{name}' is a reserved HTML element name and cannot be used as a component name
vue/no-reserved-component-names.help: Rename the component to avoid using reserved names
vue/no-template-shadow.message: Variable '{name}' shadows a variable in an outer scope
vue/no-template-shadow.help: Rename the variable to avoid shadowing
vue/no-inline-style.description: Discourage use of inline style attributes
vue/no-inline-style.help: Use CSS classes or scoped styles instead
vue/no-multi-spaces.help: Replace multiple spaces with a single space
vue/multi-word-component-names.descripti: Require component names to be multi-word
vue/multi-word-component-names.message: Component name "{name}" should be multi-word to avoid conflicts with HTML elements
vue/multi-word-component-names.help: Rename the component to use multiple words (e.g., "TodoItem" instead of "Item")
vue/a11y-img-alt.description: Require alt attribute on images for accessibility
vue/a11y-img-alt.message: elements must have an alt attribute for accessibility
vue/a11y-img-alt.help: Add alt="description" for informative images or alt="" for decorative images
vue/no-unsafe-url.description: Warn about potentially unsafe URL bindings
vue/no-unsafe-url.message: Dynamic :{attr} binding may be vulnerable to XSS via javascript: protocol
vue/no-unsafe-url.help_href: Consider using <router-link :to="..."> or sanitize URLs with @braintree/sanitize-url
vue/no-unsafe-url.help_src: Ensure URLs are sanitized before binding. Use @braintree/sanitize-url for validation
vue/no-unsafe-url.help: Ensure URLs are sanitized before binding to prevent XSS via javascript: protocol
vue/no-v-html.description: Disallow use of v-html to prevent XSS attacks
vue/no-v-html.message: v-html can lead to XSS attacks. Avoid using it with user-provided content
vue/no-v-html.help:
**Security Risk:** `v-html` renders raw HTML and can execute malicious scripts from user input. **Alternatives:** 1. Use text interpolation (auto-escaped): ```vue

{{ userContent }}

``` 2. Use a sanitization library: ```ts import DOMPurify from 'dompurify' const safeHtml = DOMPurify.sanitize(userInput) ``` 3. Use a markdown renderer with XSS protection **If you must use v-html:** - Never use with user-provided content - Only use with trusted, static content
vue/prefer-props-shorthand.description: Enforce shorthand syntax for same-name prop bindings
vue/prefer-props-shorthand.message: Use shorthand syntax for same-name prop binding
vue/attribute-hyphenation.description: Enforce attribute name casing in templates
vue/attribute-order.message: Attribute should be placed in correct order
vue/attribute-order.help: Reorder attributes to follow the recommended order
vue/no-lone-template.help: Remove the `<template>` wrapper or add a directive (v-if, v-for, v-slot)
vue/scoped-event-names.description: Suggest scoped event names for better organization
vue/scoped-event-names.message: Consider using scoped event name for better organization
vue/scoped-event-names.help: Use scoped event names like 'update:modelValue' instead of generic names
vue/component-name-in-template-casing.de: Enforce component naming style in templates
vue/no-undefined-refs.help: Make sure the variable is imported or defined in <script setup>
vue/require-scoped-style.description: Require scoped attribute on style blocks
vue/require-scoped-style.message: Style blocks should use scoped attribute to prevent style leaking
vue/require-scoped-style.help: Add the 'scoped' attribute to the style block
vapor/no-suspense.description: Warn about Suspense in Vapor-only applications
vapor/no-suspense.message: Suspense is not supported in Vapor-only applications
vapor/no-suspense.help: Suspense only works when Vapor components render inside a VDOM Suspense boundary. Use createApp with vaporInteropPlugin for Suspense support
vapor/no-inline-template.message: inline-template is deprecated and not supported in Vapor
vapor/prefer-static-class.description: Prefer static class over dynamic binding
vapor/prefer-static-class.message: Prefer static class attribute over dynamic :class binding for constant values
vapor/prefer-static-class.help: Move static classes to the class attribute
vapor/no-vue-lifecycle-events.message: @vue:{event} lifecycle events are not supported in Vapor
script/no-options-api.message: Options API is not supported in Vapor. Use Composition API instead
script/no-options-api.help: Refactor to use <script setup> with Composition API
script/no-get-current-instance.message: getCurrentInstance() returns null in Vapor components
script/no-async-in-computed.description: Disallow async functions in computed properties
script/no-async-in-computed.message: Computed properties cannot be async. They must return a value synchronously
script/no-async-in-computed.help: Use ref with watchEffect for async operations: `const data = ref(null); watchEffect(async () => { data.value = await fetchData() })`
script/no-deep-destructure-in-props.desc: Disallow deeply nested destructuring in defineProps
script/no-deep-destructure-in-props.mess: Avoid deeply nested destructuring in defineProps (max depth: {depth})
script/no-deep-destructure-in-props.help: Use simple destructuring and access nested properties via computed or direct prop access: `const props = defineProps<...>(); const nested = computed(() => props.parent.child)`
musea/require-title.description: Require title attribute in art block
musea/require-title.message: Art component requires a title attribute
musea/require-title.help: Add a title attribute to describe the component
a11y/img-alt.message: elements must have an alt attribute for accessibility
a11y/img-alt.help:
**Why:** Screen readers use alt text to describe images to visually impaired users. **For informative images:** ```vue ``` **For decorative images:** ```vue ``` **For complex images:** ```vue

Detailed description...

```
a11y/anchor-has-content.description: Require anchor elements to have accessible content
a11y/anchor-has-content.help: **Fix options:** 1. Add text content: ```vue About Us ``` 2. Add aria-label for icon-only links: ```vue ``` 3. Include image with alt: ```vue ```
a11y/heading-has-content.description: Require heading elements to have accessible content
a11y/heading-has-content.help:
**Why:** Empty headings confuse screen reader users and break document structure. **Fix:** ```vue

Section Title

```
a11y/iframe-has-title.description: Require iframe elements to have a title attribute
a11y/iframe-has-title.message: <iframe> elements must have a title attribute
a11y/iframe-has-title.help:
**Why:** Screen readers announce the title to help users understand the iframe content.

**Fix:**
```vue
<iframe 
  src="https://example.com/video"
  title="Product demo video"
></iframe>
```

**Note:** The title should describe the content, not the source.
a11y/no-distracting-elements.message: <{tag}> elements are distracting and should not be used
a11y/no-distracting-elements.help: Remove this element or use CSS animations with reduced motion support
a11y/tabindex-no-positive.help: Use tabindex="0" for focusable elements or tabindex="-1" for programmatic focus only
a11y/click-events-have-key-events.descri: Require keyboard event handlers with click events
a11y/click-events-have-key-events.messag: Non-interactive elements with @click must also have keyboard event handlers
a11y/click-events-have-key-events.help:
**Why:** Keyboard users cannot activate click-only handlers. **Best solution:** Use semantic elements: ```vue ``` **If you must use a div:** ```vue
Click me
```
a11y/form-control-has-label.description: Require form controls to have associated labels
a11y/form-control-has-label.message: <{tag}> elements must have an associated label
a11y/form-control-has-label.help:
**Option 1:** Use `<label>` with `for`:
```vue
<label for="email">Email</label>
<input id="email" type="email">
```

**Option 2:** Wrap input in label:
```vue
<label>
  Email
  <input type="email">
</label>
```

**Option 3:** Use aria-label:
```vue
<input type="search" aria-label="Search">
```

**Note:** Placeholder is NOT a substitute for a label.
a11y/form-control-has-label.help_placeho:
**Warning:** Placeholder text disappears when typing and is not accessible to all users.

**Fix:** Add a proper label:
```vue
<label for="search">Search</label>
<input id="search" placeholder="Enter keywords...">
```

Or use aria-label:
```vue
<input aria-label="Search" placeholder="Enter keywords...">
```
a11y/aria-props.help:
**Why:** Invalid ARIA attributes prevent assistive technologies from conveying the intended meaning to users.

**Valid ARIA attributes** are defined in the WAI-ARIA specification:
https://www.w3.org/TR/wai-aria/#state_prop_def

**Common ARIA attributes:**
```vue
<!-- Labeling -->
<input aria-label="Search" />
<div aria-labelledby="title-id">

<!-- State -->
<button aria-expanded="false">
<input aria-invalid="true">

<!-- Relationships -->
<input aria-describedby="hint-id">
<div aria-controls="panel-id">
```

**Note:** Custom `aria-*` attributes are not allowed. Only use attributes from the WAI-ARIA specification.
a11y/aria-props.help_suggestion:
**Invalid attribute:** `{invalid}`

**Did you mean:** `{valid}`?

This is a common typo. ARIA attributes follow specific naming conventions from the WAI-ARIA specification.
a11y/aria-role.description: Elements with ARIA roles must use a valid, non-abstract ARIA role
a11y/aria-role.message: '{role}' is not a valid ARIA role
a11y/aria-role.message_abstract: '{role}' is an abstract ARIA role and cannot be used directly
a11y/aria-role.help:
**Why:** Invalid ARIA roles prevent assistive technologies from conveying the intended meaning to users. **Valid ARIA roles** are defined in the WAI-ARIA specification: https://www.w3.org/TR/wai-aria/#role_definitions **Common roles:** ```vue
Click me
Modal content
Toggle
...
...
...
Title
...
```
a11y/aria-role.help_abstract:
**Abstract roles** are base concepts in the ARIA spec and cannot be used directly. **Abstract roles include:** `command`, `composite`, `input`, `landmark`, `range`, `roletype`, `section`, `sectionhead`, `select`, `structure`, `widget`, `window` **Use concrete roles instead:** ```vue
...
...
...
...
```
a11y/aria-role.help_suggestion:
**Invalid role:** `{invalid}`

**Did you mean:** `{valid}`?

Refer to the WAI-ARIA specification for a complete list of valid roles.
a11y/no-aria-hidden-on-focusable.message: aria-hidden="true" must not be used on focusable elements
a11y/no-aria-hidden-on-focusable.help: Remove aria-hidden="true" or make the element non-focusable with tabindex="-1"
a11y/no-access-key.description: Disallow the use of the accesskey attribute
a11y/no-access-key.message: The accesskey attribute should not be used. Access keys create keyboard shortcut conflicts
a11y/no-access-key.help: Remove the accesskey attribute. Access keys create inconsistent keyboard shortcuts across platforms and conflict with assistive technology shortcuts
a11y/no-autofocus.description: Disallow the use of the autofocus attribute
a11y/no-autofocus.message: The autofocus attribute should not be used. It can disrupt navigation for screen reader users
a11y/no-autofocus.help: Remove the autofocus attribute. Manage focus programmatically when needed using ref and focus()
a11y/no-role-presentation-on-focusable.d: Disallow role="presentation" or role="none" on focusable elements
a11y/no-role-presentation-on-focusable.m: Focusable elements must not have role="presentation" or role="none"
a11y/no-role-presentation-on-focusable.h: Remove the role attribute or make the element non-focusable. Presentation role removes semantic meaning while the element remains focusable
a11y/aria-unsupported-elements.descripti: Disallow ARIA attributes on elements that do not support them
a11y/aria-unsupported-elements.help: Remove the ARIA attribute or role. Elements like <meta>, <html>, <script>, and <style> are not rendered and do not support ARIA
a11y/no-redundant-roles.message: <{tag}> has an implicit role of '{role}'. The role attribute is redundant
a11y/no-redundant-roles.help: Remove the redundant role attribute. The element already has this role implicitly through its HTML semantics
a11y/mouse-events-have-key-events.descri: Require focus/blur events with mouse events
a11y/mouse-events-have-key-events.messag: @mouseenter/@mouseover must be paired with @focus for keyboard accessibility
@mouseleave/@mouseout must be paired with @blur for keyboard accessibility
a11y/alt-text.description: Require alternative text for media elements
a11y/alt-text.message_img: elements must have an alt attribute
a11y/alt-text.message_area: <area> elements must have alt, aria-label, or aria-labelledby
a11y/alt-text.message_input_image: <input type="image"> must have alt, aria-label, or aria-labelledby
a11y/alt-text.message_object: <object> elements must have title, aria-label, or aria-labelledby
a11y/alt-text.help_img: Add an alt attribute: `` or `` for decorative images
a11y/alt-text.help_area: Add alt, aria-label, or aria-labelledby to the <area> element
a11y/alt-text.help_input_image: Add alt, aria-label, or aria-labelledby to the <input type="image"> element
a11y/alt-text.help_object: Add title, aria-label, or aria-labelledby to the <object> element
a11y/anchor-is-valid.description: Enforce valid href on anchor elements
a11y/anchor-is-valid.message_empty: <a> element has an empty href attribute
a11y/anchor-is-valid.message_hash: <a> element has href="#" which is not a valid navigation target
a11y/anchor-is-valid.message_javascript: <a> element has a javascript: href which is not recommended
a11y/anchor-is-valid.message_missing: <a> element is missing an href attribute
a11y/anchor-is-valid.help: **Fix:** ```vue About Link ```
a11y/label-has-for.description: Require labels to have associated form controls
a11y/label-has-for.message: <label> must have a `for` attribute or wrap a form control (input, select, textarea)
a11y/label-has-for.help:
**Fix options:**

1. Use `for` attribute:
```vue
<label for="name">Name</label>
<input id="name" type="text">
```

2. Wrap form control:
```vue
<label>Name <input type="text"></label>
```
a11y/interactive-supports-focus.descript: Require interactive role elements to be focusable
a11y/interactive-supports-focus.message: Element with role="{role}" must be focusable (add tabindex="0")
a11y/interactive-supports-focus.help:
**Why:** Interactive roles imply the element can be activated by keyboard users. **Fix:** ```vue
Click me
``` **Better:** Use native interactive elements: ```vue ```
a11y/role-has-required-aria-props.descri: Require ARIA roles to have required properties
a11y/role-has-required-aria-props.messag: Elements with role="{role}" must have the '{prop}' attribute
a11y/media-has-caption.description: Require media elements to have captions
a11y/media-has-caption.message: <{tag}> elements must have a <track kind="captions"> for accessibility
a11y/media-has-caption.help:
**Fix:**
```vue
<video src="movie.mp4">
  <track kind="captions" src="captions.vtt" />
</video>
```

**Alternatives:**
- Add `muted` attribute for decorative media
- Add `aria-label` for described media
a11y/no-static-element-interactions.desc: Disallow event handlers on static elements
a11y/no-static-element-interactions.mess: <{tag}> is a static element and should not have interactive event handlers
a11y/no-static-element-interactions.help:
**Why:** Non-interactive elements (div, span, etc.) should not have click/keyboard handlers without a role. **Fix options:** 1. Use a native interactive element: ```vue ``` 2. Add role and tabindex: ```vue
Click me
```
vue/use-unique-element-ids.description: Enforce unique element IDs using useId() instead of static literals
vue/use-unique-element-ids.message: Static id="{value}" may cause duplicate IDs when component is rendered multiple times
vue/use-unique-element-ids.message_refer: Static ID reference '{value}' may reference a non-unique ID
vue/use-unique-element-ids.help:
**Why:** Static `id` attributes can cause duplicate IDs when components are rendered multiple times, leading to accessibility issues and broken functionality (e.g., label-input associations).

**Fix:** Use `useId()` from Vue 3.5+:
```vue
<script setup>
import { useId } from 'vue'

const id = useId()
</script>

<template>
  <label :for="id">Name:</label>
  <input :id="id" type="text">
</template>
```

**Benefits:**
- Generates unique IDs for each component instance
- Stable across server and client rendering (SSR-safe)
- Works with labels, ARIA attributes, and form associations
vue/use-unique-element-ids.help_has_use_:
**Good:** You're already using `useId()`. Now use the generated ID in your template:
```vue
<label :for="id">Name:</label>
<input :id="id" type="text">
```

Replace static `id`, `for`, and ARIA ID references with the dynamic value.
vue/no-child-content.description: Disallow child content when using v-html or v-text
vue/no-child-content.message: Child content of element with v-{directive} will be overwritten
vue/no-child-content.help:
**Why:** When using `v-html` or `v-text`, Vue replaces the element's child content with the directive value. Any child content is silently ignored. **Fix:** Remove the child content: ```vue
This will be ignored
```
vue/valid-attribute-name.help: Attribute names must not contain spaces, quotes, `>`, `/`, `=`, or control characters
vue/no-v-text-v-html-on-component.descri: Disallow v-text / v-html on component elements
vue/no-v-text-v-html-on-component.messag: v-{directive} cannot be used on component <{tag}>
vue/no-v-text-v-html-on-component.help:
**Why:** Components have their own rendering logic. `v-text`/`v-html` would overwrite the component's content unexpectedly.

**Fix:** Pass content via props or slots:
```vue
<!-- Instead of -->
<MyComponent v-html="content" />

<!-- Use slots -->
<MyComponent><div v-html="content" /></MyComponent>
```
vue/require-component-is.help:
**Why:** The `<component>` element is a dynamic component placeholder. Without `is`, Vue cannot determine which component to render.

**Fix:**
```vue
<component :is="currentComponent" />
<component is="MyComponent" />
```
vue/no-useless-template-attributes.messa: '{attr}' is useless on <template> — it will be ignored
vue/no-useless-template-attributes.help:
**Why:** `<template>` is not rendered to the DOM. Only structural directives (v-if, v-for, v-slot) and `:key` are meaningful.

**Fix:** Move attributes to a child element or remove them.
vue/valid-v-memo.missing_expression: v-memo requires a dependency array expression
vue/valid-v-memo.unexpected_argument: v-memo does not accept an argument
vue/valid-v-memo.help:
**Usage:** ```vue
``` **Note:** `v-memo` accepts an array of dependencies, similar to React's `useMemo`.
vue/use-v-on-exact.description: Enforce .exact modifier on v-on when using system modifiers
vue/use-v-on-exact.message: @{event} without .exact will also fire when system modifiers are pressed
vue/use-v-on-exact.help:
**Why:** `@click` fires even when Ctrl/Shift/Alt/Meta is pressed. Use `.exact` to only fire when no system modifiers are active.

**Fix:**
```vue
<!-- Before -->
<button @click="onClick" @click.ctrl="onCtrlClick" />

<!-- After -->
<button @click.exact="onClick" @click.ctrl="onCtrlClick" />
```
vue/v-slot-style.help:
**Shorthand:** `<template #header>` (default)
**Longform:** `<template v-slot:header>`

Choose one style and be consistent.
vue/prop-name-casing.description: Enforce kebab-case prop names in templates
vue/prop-name-casing.help:
Use kebab-case for prop names in templates:
```vue
<!-- Bad -->
<MyComponent myProp="value" />

<!-- Good -->
<MyComponent my-prop="value" />
```
vue/html-quotes.description: Enforce quotes style of HTML attributes
vue/html-quotes.message_double: Expected double quotes but found single quotes
vue/html-quotes.message_single: Expected single quotes but found double quotes
vue/html-quotes.help: Use consistent quote style for HTML attribute values.
vue/component-definition-name-casing.des: Enforce PascalCase for component file names
vue/component-definition-name-casing.mes: Component file name '{name}' should be PascalCase
vue/component-definition-name-casing.hel:
Rename the file to PascalCase:
```
my-component.vue -> MyComponent.vue
myComponent.vue  -> MyComponent.vue
```
cli.suggest_split: Consider splitting large components into smaller ones
cli.suggest_template: Large template detected ({size} bytes). Consider extracting parts into child components
ssr/no-browser-globals-in-ssr.descriptio: Disallow browser-only globals in SSR context
ssr/no-browser-globals-in-ssr.message: '{name}' is a browser-only global and is not available in SSR
ssr/no-browser-globals-in-ssr.help: Move browser-only code to client lifecycle hooks like onMounted() or use <ClientOnly>
ssr/no-hydration-mismatch.description: Disallow non-deterministic values that cause hydration mismatch
ssr/no-hydration-mismatch.message: '{pattern}' may cause hydration mismatch between server and client
ssr/no-hydration-mismatch.message-attr: '{pattern}' in attribute binding may cause hydration mismatch
ssr/no-hydration-mismatch.help: Use useId() for unique IDs, or wrap in <ClientOnly> for client-only values
ts/2322.message: Type '{source}' is not assignable to type '{target}'
ts/2322.help:
**Type mismatch detected.**

You're trying to assign a value of type `{source}` to something that expects `{target}`.

**Common fixes:**
- Check if the value matches the expected type
- Use type assertion if you're sure: `value as {target}`
- Update the type definition to accept the actual type

**Example:**
```ts
// If target is 'number' but source is 'string'
const value = parseInt(stringValue); // Convert string to number
```
ts/2339.message: Property '{name}' does not exist on type '{type}'
ts/2345.message: Argument of type '{source}' is not assignable to parameter of type '{target}'
ts/2345.help:
**Argument type mismatch.**

The function expects `{target}` but received `{source}`.

**Solutions:**
- Convert the value to the expected type
- Check if you're passing the correct argument
- Update the function signature if needed

```ts
// Example: function expects number
func(Number(value)); // Convert to number
```
ts/2555.help:
**Too many arguments provided.**

The function only accepts **{expected}** argument(s) but you passed **{actual}**.

**Solution:**
Remove the extra arguments or update the function to accept more parameters.
ts/2307.message: Cannot find module '{module}' or its corresponding type declarations
ts/2307.help:
**Module not found: `{module}`**

**Possible causes:**
- The package is not installed
- The import path is incorrect
- Type declarations are missing

**Solutions:**
```bash
# 1. Install the package
npm install {module}

# 2. Install type declarations (if separate)
npm install -D @types/{module}
```

**For local modules:**
- Check the file path is correct
- Use relative paths: `./` or `../`
- Check file extension
ts/2300.help:
**Duplicate declaration of `{name}`.**

This identifier is already declared in this scope.

**Solutions:**
- Rename one of the declarations
- Remove the duplicate
- Check if you're accidentally importing the same thing twice
ts/2741.message: Property '{name}' is missing in type '{source}' but required in type '{target}'
ts/2344.message: Type '{type}' does not satisfy the constraint '{constraint}'
ts/2344.help:
**Type constraint violation.**

The type `{type}` doesn't meet the requirements of `{constraint}`.

**Solution:**
Ensure your type extends or implements the required constraint:
```ts
// Example: T must extend SomeBase
function example<T extends SomeBase>(value: T) { }
```
ts/2351.help:
**Cannot use `new` on this value.**

This expression cannot be used with `new`.

**Common causes:**
- It's not a class or constructor function
- You're trying to instantiate a type, not a class

**Example:**
```ts
// Wrong: type alias, not a class
type MyType = { x: number };
new MyType(); // Error!

// Correct: use class
class MyClass { x: number = 0; }
new MyClass(); // OK
```
ts/vue/9004.help:
**Slot `{name}` usage error.**

**Common issues:**
- Slot doesn't exist on the component
- Wrong slot props

**Usage:**
```vue
<template>
  <MyComponent>
    <template #{name}="{ prop }">
      <!-- slot content -->
    </template>
  </MyComponent>
</template>
```
a11y/no-i-for-icon.message: <i> element with icon CSS class should not be used for icons. Use a semantic element instead
a11y/no-i-for-icon.help: **Why:** The `` element semantically represents *italic text*, not icons. Using it for icon fonts creates accessibility issues — screen readers may announce meaningless content. **Fix:** Use `` with `aria-hidden="true"` instead: ```vue Home ``` Or use an SVG icon component for best accessibility.
a11y/no-refer-to-non-existent-id.message: The {kind} attribute references id="{id}" which does not exist in the template
a11y/no-refer-to-non-existent-id.help:
**Why:** ID reference attributes like `for`, `aria-labelledby`, `aria-describedby`, etc. must point to an existing element with that `id`. A broken reference silently breaks the association.

**Fix:** Ensure the referenced ID exists in the template:
```vue
<label for="name">Name:</label>
<input id="name" type="text">
```
vue/use-unique-element-ids.message_form: Form element has static id="{value}" — use useId() for unique IDs to ensure label/input associations work correctly
vue/use-unique-element-ids.message_aria_: Static id="{value}" on element with ARIA references — use useId() to ensure ARIA associations remain unique
vue/permitted-contents.block_in_inline: <{child}> (block element) is not allowed inside <{parent}> (phrasing content only)
vue/permitted-contents.interactive_nesti: <{tag}> is an interactive element and cannot be nested inside another interactive element
vue/permitted-contents.invalid_child: <{child}> is not allowed as a direct child of <{parent}>
vue/permitted-contents.help:
**Why:** HTML has content model rules that define which elements can be nested inside others. Violating these rules causes browsers to auto-correct the DOM, leading to unexpected rendering. **Common rules:** - `

`, ``, `` can only contain phrasing (inline) content - `` and `
html/deprecated-element.help: This element is obsolete per the HTML Living Standard. Use modern HTML elements with CSS for styling instead.
html/no-consecutive-br.help:
Use

elements or CSS margin/padding for spacing instead of multiple
tags.
html/id-duplication.help: Element IDs must be unique within a document. Duplicate IDs break label associations, ARIA references, and getElementById().
html/require-datetime.message: <time> element needs a datetime attribute when content is not a valid datetime string
html/require-datetime.help: Add a datetime attribute with a machine-readable date/time value, e.g. <time datetime="2024-12-25">Christmas</time>
html/no-empty-palpable-content.message: <{tag}> element is empty but expects visible content
html/no-empty-palpable-content.help: Add text content, child elements, or use aria-label for accessible content.
html/no-duplicate-dt.help: Each term in a definition list should be unique. Combine definitions under a single <dt> or use distinct terms.
a11y/heading-levels.help: Heading levels should increase by one. Screen reader users navigate by heading levels, so skipping levels makes the document structure confusing.
a11y/use-list.message: Text appears to be a list item. Use <ul> or <ol> for proper list semantics
a11y/use-list.help: Screen readers cannot identify bullet characters as list items. Use semantic <ul>/<ol> with <li> elements instead.
a11y/placeholder-label-option.message: Placeholder <option> should have disabled or hidden attribute
a11y/placeholder-label-option.help:
Add disabled or hidden to the placeholder <option> to prevent it from being selected:
<option value="" disabled>Choose one</option>
a11y/landmark-roles.duplicate_main: Only one <main> landmark is allowed per page
a11y/landmark-roles.help_duplicate_main: A page should have exactly one <main> landmark. Remove duplicate <main> elements or use role="main" on only one element.
a11y/landmark-roles.missing_label: Multiple "{role}" landmarks found without distinct labels
a11y/landmark-roles.help_missing_label: When multiple landmarks of the same type exist, each should have a unique aria-label or aria-labelledby to help users distinguish them.
vue/no-boolean-attr-value.message: Boolean attribute "{attr}" should not have value "{value}"
vue/no-boolean-attr-value.help: Remove the value. Use just {attr} instead of {attr}="...".
